texto: I have learned that if you fake your life it will kill you slowly, terribly and cause the most pain for everyone. You try to do the right thing, not hurt anyone, conform to what love is supposed to be and what it is supposed to look like, but in the end it destroys you. It eats away at your heart and soul like acid. You slowly destroy those around you that you say you love, and you slowly destroy yourself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark D'Arbanville: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I have learned that if you fake your life it will kill you slowly, terribly and cause the most pa…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, being-true-to-yourself, honesty pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e honesty.
